question.text: Which of the following statements about the NISM Series XV certification is CORRECT?

answer.text: It is optional for persons engaged as research analysts under SEBI regulations
It is required for associated persons registered under SEBI (Research Analyst) Regulations, 2014
It is only required for sell-side analysts, not buy-side analysts
It needs to be completed only once with no renewal requirement

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An associated person shall be required to pass the NISM-Series-XV: Research Analyst Certification Examination to fulfill the requirements specified under Regulation 7(2) of the SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations, 2014. **The Trap:** Option A is incorrect — the certification is mandatory, not optional. Option C is wrong — it applies to all associated persons regardless of whether they are sell-side or buy-side. Option D is incorrect — NISM certifications require periodic renewal through Continuing Professional Education (CPE). **Cross-Exam Context:** Similar certification requirements exist across all NISM exams — Series V-A for MF distributors, Series X-A for investment advisers, etc. The regulatory mandate for certification is a common thread. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Certification validity and CPE requirements should be tracked carefully. Lapsed certifications can result in regulatory action against both the individual and the registered entity.
